NY PRODUCT DESIGN AWARDS
Honoring Talented Product Designers, Design Teams & Manufacturers Worldwide.

The NY Product Design Awards was launched to honor the efforts of talented product designers, design teams, and manufacturers from all over the world, whose designs have made daily living that much better with their practical and ingenious creations.

Recognizing that excellence exists in various forms in the design industry, the NY Product Design Awards intends to celebrate excellence in all its facets that are found in design work and its designers.

A well-thought-out design is both functional and aesthetically pleasing, and all-in-all improves one’s daily life. Said designs can stem from different people’s personal projects, commercial engagements or researches. To the NY Product Design Awards, these are the individuals we wish to honor.

FEATURED JURY MEMBERS

Meet the world-class creatives, designers, and experts who formed the NY Product Design Awards jury panel.

The Awards is judged by members of the IAA Judging Council, a group of leading industry professionals. Showcase your brilliant projects and capture the attention of our esteemed panel of judges today!

Australia

Alexandre Collares

Creative Art Director, Ogilvy Australia

Alexandre de Mello Collares is a prominent creative art director of Ogilvy Australia.

Collares possesses more than a decade of experience in the advertising industry, having worked in both large and mid-sized agencies in the Australian and Brazilian markets, including DDB, McCann, Geometry Global, and Fox Sports, among others. Throughout his career, he has had the opportunity to collaborate with esteemed clients such as KFC, Pizza Hut, Coca-Cola, McDonald's, Fox Sports, SC Johnson, Westpac, and numerous others, allowing him to accumulate knowledge and exchange insights with a diverse and talented group of professionals.

He remains consistently focused and self-motivated, excelling both individually and in teamwork. With a profound passion for creative ideas and meticulous execution, he anticipates that his finest project is yet to come.

Bulgaria

Vasil Velchev

Media & Product Life Cycle Manager, Europe, Ideal Standard International NV

Vasil Velchev has amassed over a decade of experience as a graphic designer within global organizations. Initially, his foray into product design was more of a hobby, but as his creations gained international recognition and garnered awards, his confidence in his ideas grew.

Several years ago, He initiated the MAGMA project, a platform aimed at consolidating his diverse creations under one umbrella. The project's name is symbolic, representing the designer's belief that every original idea should remain in a constant "liquid" state, continuously evolving to explore its full potential and staying vibrant until it transforms into a tangible product.

Velchev also serves as a valued member of the Grand Jury for prestigious awards. His remarkable talent has earned him approximately 25 international awards across various categories, with a primary focus on furniture design, recognized by design awards worldwide.


United States

Yafei Zhang

Associate Principal / BIM Lead / Adjunct Professor, Kohn Pedersen Fox; Kean University

Yafei Zhang, AIA, LEED AP, is a licensed New Jersey architect and Associate Principal at Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) with over a decade of experience leading corporate interiors and mixed-use projects from concept through construction.

Yafei’s expertise spans the full project lifecycle, from feasibility and design development to construction and post-occupancy evaluation. She leads multidisciplinary consultant teams to deliver high-performance, sustainable interiors for leading corporate clients, including Meta, ensuring projects meet design, budget, and schedule goals.

Her notable projects include Meta 50 Hudson Yards, the Bund Fosun Center, and the University of Michigan Center for Innovation, reflecting her ability to turn complex requirements into cohesive, high-performing spaces. With strong expertise in Revit and BIM-driven workflows, Yafei drives project efficiency through data-informed coordination and delivery. She also serves as National Education Lead (North America) and Regional Lead (New York) for Women in BIM, advocating for leadership diversity and innovation across the AEC industry.

Virtual Recognition + Winner Highlights
NY AWARDS STATUETTE

Ava: A Symbol of Design Excellence & Creation

Inspired by Galatea, a statue carved of ivory coming to life, the statuette is shaped in the form of a female humanoid, with elements that strongly affirms its non-biological origins.

The statuette is titled “Ava”, a variation of Eve, the first woman found in Abrahamic religions. Eve came to be as soon as life was breathed into her. In that regard, we recognize that designers are able to breathe life into their works, and give way to something truly great.

The design of the statuette is aimed to invoke a sense of pristine clarity with its frame clad in ivory white, while its golden embellishments effectively communicate a sense of elegance and divinity.

All of these elements culminate in symbols of excellence, creation, and the unbounded future that is in store. Much like Pygmalion – the sculptor of Galatea, whose excellence shaped his future, we too hope that this statuette symbolizes designers whose works shape the path they walk in their own futures.

LIT ENTERTAINMENT AWARDS
Evolving with the Times – From Commercial to Entertainment.

LIT Entertainment Awards recognizes creative and advertising excellence in the global entertainment industry - from the silver screen to interactive gaming, from television content to wide-encompassing digital entertainments. Previously LIT Commercial Awards, LIT now transitions from its previous roots, expanding its reach to celebrate all facets of entertainment advertising and marketing, eagerly embracing this evolution to accommodate a broader spectrum of entertainment.

The LIT Entertainment Awards is open to all creative and marketing professionals, designers, agencies, and every creative in between. Whether you're reaching out to a local audience or making waves internationally, LIT is committed to spotlight advertising brilliance of entertainment on a grander stage than ever before!

ENTER LIT ENTERTAINMENT AWARDS

CATEGORIES
Celebrating the Spectrum of Creative Brilliance in Entertainment Across All Platforms Globally.

LIT Entertainment Awards salutes innovation, storytelling, and design, honoring an array of advertising and creative ideas that captivate audiences worldwide. From film, television, games, home, live or digital entertainment, music, and marketing, to the masterminds crafting these memorable experiences behind the scenes — LIT recognizes it all.

LITO STATUETTE
Icon of Entertainment Excellence

The LITO statuette stands as the emblematic heart of the LIT Entertainment Awards, radiating an enigmatic energy that signifies the boundless realm of entertainment advertising and marketing.

Dressed in its distinctive polka dot garb, LITO embodies the endless realms of imagination, encouraging participants across films, television, games, and all entertainment mediums to venture beyond the ordinary. It's a reminder to never lose sight of one's original goals, even as they craft fresh narratives and pioneering experiences.

LITO's base implements a refined and elegant finish, creating a stark contrast against its gleaming black and pristine white visage. It serves as a beacon, urging entertainment visionaries globally to shatter their own boundaries, aspiring not just to participate in the industry but to redefine it.

VIEW LITO STATUETTE

FEATURED JURORS

At the LIT Entertainment Awards, each and every submission is evaluated only by the best in the industry, comprising a collective of industry trailblazers and visionaries in the Grand Jury Panel. By presenting your masterpieces here, you're not just competing; you're sharing your ideas with some of the most esteemed minds in entertainment, impressing, inspiring, and resonating with an elite panel of individuals.

Brazil

Nayla Alana

Associate Creative Director, LePub

Nayla Alana is an Associate Creative Director at LePub Brazil.
As an Associate Creative Director at LePub Brazil, Nayla crafts bold, insightful, and human-centred ideas that connect brands with audiences in meaningful ways. With over 14 years of experience, she brings a rich creative background shaped by her time at leading agencies such as Zulu Alpha Kilo Toronto, Galeria, Ogilvy Brazil, J. Walter Thompson, Publicis, and DDB Spain.

She is highly knowledgeable in brand communication with expertise spanning concept development, storytelling, and integrated campaign execution. She has crafted impactful campaigns for leading global brands, including Heineken, Jeep, TikTok, Adidas, Kotex, Ford, Nestlé, Netflix, Bradesco, Dove, and others.

United Kingdom

Michael Boszko

Creative Director, Havas Play London

Michael Boszko is a Creative Director at Iris, creating work for leading cultural, sports, and entertainment brands.

Michael began his career in Dubai, writing for Nike, and has since garnered recognition at all major global festivals through his work with leading network agencies. Today, he leads Iris London’s Creative Council and also serves as a member of the agency’s Global Council, helping to shape creative vision and drive innovation across the network. His clients have included global names such as Samsung, Nike, HSBC, Pizza Hut, and Starbucks.

His expertise in cultural, sports, and entertainment branding makes him a discerning and insightful judge, well-positioned to evaluate work with both professional rigor and an appreciation for impactful storytelling.

United States

Jenn Szekely

Managing Partner, USA, Coley Porter Bell

Jenn leads Coley Porter Bell’s US business, based in New York and is a member of the Coley Porter Bell Board. She brings over 20 years of B2C and B2B branding and marketing experience across a variety of industries, including retail, technology, hospitality, FMCG, nonprofit, manufacturing and healthcare.

Prior to joining Coley Porter Bell, Jenn was Executive Director, Marketing & New Business for FutureBrand where she led the marketing and new business team in North America. As part of her role, she helped expand the business to the West Coast and worked with clients such as Cisco, Peloton, Honeywell, Tupperware, Hard Rock International, Microsoft and Forever 21.

Previously, Jenn was Senior Vice President at Siegel+Gale, working in both New York and London with clients such as PepsiCo, Target, Carter’s, J.Crew, Choice Hotels, Procter & Gamble, Sony, and Seamless GrubHub. A highlight of her time at Siegel+Gale was working on the branding of Jeff Bezos’ aerospace company, Blue Origin, where she had the opportunity to work with the Bezos family and learn about the amazing world of space travel.

Jenn’s experience spans a variety of strategic and creative initiatives, from brand strategy, brand architecture and naming to creative executions including, visual identity, packaging, digital and trade show experience to hotel and retail interior design.

Jenn has been a judge at the North American Transform Awards and before her career in branding, she ran her own company, which included two retail stores in Boston, Massachusetts, specializing in fashion, art, antiques and design that won numerous awards and received critical acclaim in national newspapers, magazines, books and television.

United States

Vasily Petrenko

CEO & Co-Founder, Another World VR Corporation

Vasily Petrenko is the CEO and co-founder of Another World VR Corp, a global leader in free-roam virtual reality experiences for location-based entertainment centers worldwide.

With a background in Cytology and Genetics from Novosibirsk State University, Vasily transitioned early into tech and entertainment, co-founding ventures that combine science, innovation, and storytelling. His journey began in 2014 with Time Quest Lab, supporting escape rooms, theme parks, and interactive exhibitions. As CFO, he managed forecasting, marketing, and growth, and at Another World, he has led expansion, rebranding, and capital raising while redefining how users engage with technology in physical spaces.

His expertise in strategy, investment, sales, and product–market fit, particularly within emerging tech sectors, gives him a sharp perspective for evaluating startups shaping the future.

FAQ
ENTRY SUBMISSION, ELIGIBILITIES AND CATEGORIES

How to submit entries?

Entry is simple. Submission is online and is completed in 4 simple steps:

Register - or login if you are already a registrant.
Fill in the simple online submission form – remember you can select multiple categories for each entry.
Upload JPEG, PNG, PDF files or URL, as needed.
Add to Cart. Then, add more entries / categories or checkout and pay.

Entries may be submitted in more than one category at an additional cost. No additional materials are needed for the same entry. In a few, such as campaigns that include a plan or report, submit the materials you presented to your client. If one is not available, offer a brief summary of objectives, challenges and solutions.

Submissions

Entries must be produced within the last FIVE (5) calendar years up through the final deadline.
All entries must be submitted for judging exactly as published or implemented and may not be modified for the competition. The NY Product Design Awards may request proof of initial production and/or publication date.
The awards reserve the right to re-categorize incorrectly classified entries.
Judges reserve the right to withhold an award in a category if submissions do not meet their expectations.

Who is eligible? (Solo and/or Team)

The NY Product Design Awards is open to:

Advertising Agencies
Art Directors
Brand / Business Owner
Car Designers
Creative Boutiques
Design Firms
Designers
Engineers
Entrepreneurs
Fashion Designers
Freelance Professionals
Furniture Designers
Graphic Designers
In-house Marketing
Institutions
Manufacturers
Product Designers
PR firms
Students
Teachers
Anyone aged 18 and with a great new product design

How long should my entry brief be?

It is recommended that you keep your synopsis to approximately 350 words. We suggest you utilize this section of your entry to elaborate on the piece as well as the creativity and originality of your idea. The brief should strengthen your entry, as well as providing the jury a better understanding of your project. A written translation or adaptation must accompany non-English entries.

Is there a limit to the number of entries I can submit?

There are no limits on the number of entries you can submit.

Can I enter my work into multiple categories?

Yes, you can enter the same work into as many categories as you see fit. There will be an additional fee for each category.

Can I submit a work that has won an award or has been entered in previous NY Product Design Awards, or in another competition?

Yes, you may submit work that has been previously submitted or has won an award. However, in an effort to promote the creation of new work and maintain a fresh flow of ideas, we encourage you to submit new work.

Can I send a sample of my product to the competition? If so, will it be returned to me and do I need to send a return-handling fee?

It is not required that you send actual samples of your product to the competition. Images and a description of the product are sufficient.

How will I know if my submission is complete, and what do I do if it’s not?

If a submission is incomplete at the closing of the competition (e.g. the submission has not been paid, or incorrectly uploaded images), it will not be eligible. Although we make every effort to ensure the completion of submissions, we are not responsible for incomplete entries, thus being unable to offer a refund should an entry be incomplete at the time of the closing of the competition.

Can I make changes to my entry after I have paid?

Yes, you may edit your entry until the start of the judging period.

Can we enter as a team?

Yes. You can enter as an individual or as a team. You can credit the team members under the Credit column.

What is "Keep My Entry Private"?

If this is selected, your entry brief and images will not be shown to the public after it has been crowned as a NY Product Design Awards winner. You can only see the entry thumbnail when you browse the Winner's Page. We will not promote any private winning entries on our social media platforms as well.

SUBMISSION FORMAT

Image File

The submissions should be saved in a flattened JPEG or JPG format (please keep each file under 4MB, 1,200px wide is preferable, 300dpi, RGB).

What if I have trouble uploading my images?

Firstly, check if the image does not exceed the maximum size (4MB) or width (1,200 px), and the file name only contains letters or numbers. Once checked, try to upload your images again. If you’re still having trouble after this, contact our team at info@nydesignawards.com.

Audio/Video File

Upload video/audio to Vimeo/YouTube/Youku.
Paste the URL link into the URL field.

Entries with audio/video must be submitted with a direct URL that will take judges directly to the work. If it is password-protected, you must provide a username and password for judging purposes. Web Videos entered are judged solely on the content provided. Upload a PDF brief if explanation is required before evaluation of the work.

Please provide the direct URL link and any required username or password.

ENTRY FEES AND PAYMENT

Entry Fees

Standard Entry is $219 for first entry of work. If a work is submitted in more than one category, an additional entry fee for each category is required. Each entrant is charged a $30 annual administrative fee.

Entry fees will not be refundable once submitted. Withdrawn entries (whether withdrawn by entrant or by the Awards for the entrant’s failure to comply with entry rules) will NOT be refunded as well. Please click here to see entry fees.

How can I pay for the entry that I submit?

Online. You may pay for your entry after the submission of your works through Credit Card (VISA / MasterCard) or PayPal (AMEX / PayPal).

Are all the fees on your website in USD?

Yes, we only accept USD at this moment.

JUDGING

The juries

The prestige of the NY Product Design Awards competition relies in large part on the high caliber of the juries, whom we vetted to review submissions. The jury panel comprised of experienced professionals in their field and industry, including creative designers and art directors from international agencies, product designers, educators, marketers, manufacturers or business owners, and exemplify diversity in professional experience, geography, gender, and ethnicity. All judges are selected based on experience, availability and credibility to their respective industry.

Judging criteria

Judges score on a scale of 1-100, based on the below criteria:

Form
Design’s inherent visual and artistic quality
Aesthetics: desirability of the design’s overall composition,
Does the design appeal to target market?
Does the design elicit emotional responses from users?
Does the design inform users of its function intuitively?
Creativity & Innovation
General evaluation of design idea, and its employment of creative or innovative concepts
Aesthetics: desirability of the design’s overall composition,
Uniqueness of design?
Catering to untapped market?
Improving on existing product or service already in the market?
Finishing & Functionality
Product quality, usability, safety features, ease of maintenance
Is the product well-made?
Does the product’s quality fit the price point?
How well does the product perform as designed?
Does the product come with safety considerations?
With the above in mind, is production well thought-out?
Impact
Benefits, or lack thereof, the design brings to users and their environment
Any positive impact, either culturally or industrially?
Does the design push the benchmark of quality?
Any direct or indirect contributions to a better world?

The NY Product Design Awards and International Awards Associate Inc's (IAA) decision are final in its sole discretion and binding in relation to all matters relating to Competitions and may not be subject to any discussions or correspondence.

Judging process

Judging is blind. All entrant information is removed from entries to prevent bias/conflict of interest.

The NY Product Design Awards' judging process is designed to be impartial and free from outside influences by withholding both the names of the judges on its panel as well as the contestant's names throughout the entire evaluation process. Both judge and contestant names are withheld until after award winning entries are selected. This anonymity shelters all involved from any vested interest which may attempt to influence the judges' opinions or the judging process.

WINNERS

How we choose NY Product Design Awards winners?

Entries are judged at random without comparison to other entries in a category. Work is evaluated on criteria determined by the job's requirements (see Judging Criteria). Freelancers are not compared to design firms in the same category.

To ensure high standards of high quality, a category may have multiple winners or no winners. Work is critiqued rated by consensus, with the panel of judges determining a rating on a scale of 100 points.

Entries scored between 85 and 100 points are Gold Winners.
Entries scored between 60 and 84 points are Silver Winners.

Entries receiving an average score under 60 do not receive an award.

Levels of winning and number of winning entries

There are FOUR (4) levels of achievement in the competition:

Product Design of the Year
Product Designer of the Year
Gold: Average score between 85 - 100
Silver: Average score between 60 – 84

The number of awards presented by the NY Product Design Awards is determined by the number of submissions in a given category, the scoring range and the number of tie scores.

How do I know if I’ve won an award?

All winners for the 2026 competition will be announced on May 15, 2026 via the NY Product Design Awards’ official page. All registered participants can then login to see the winning works.

We will duly notify all award winners by email. Do ensure that your email address is correct, as our notifications may get caught in spam filters or receive a non-deliverable return. You may change your email address by logging into your account and updating your information.

What do the winners receive?

Each year, the NY Product Design Awards honors the best in creativity from around the globe, while you give us a glimpse into the future of the profession.

The Product Design / Designer of the Year Winners will receive a complimentary limited-edition trophy and professionally designed certificate.
One professionally designed certificate commemorating the winning entry, which includes entry title, entering company/winners' name and category. Decal will be affixed indicating the level of win.
Top tier (Gold and Silver) winners will be featured on NY Product Design Awards site's Gallery, where professionals can view your work.
A press release template sent to major industry outlets, celebrating your win.
Professionally designed logos for use on websites, in email signatures, etc.
In addition to the complimentary certificates, Gold and Silver winners may purchase beautiful Ava statuettes, and/or additional customized certificates.
Gold and Silver winners will get featured by the NY Product Design Awards on social media or our media partner on Muse.World’s website.

Why must winners’ statuettes be purchased?

The NY Product Design Awards’ mission is to recognize outstanding creativity in firms that otherwise are overshadowed by large firms' budgets, which dominate most other marketing communications competitions. We also want to extend and encourage students to enter and compete on their entering the profession of digital media.

The NY Product Design Awards’ mission is to recognize outstanding creativity in firms that otherwise are overshadowed by large firms' budgets, which dominate most other marketing communications competitions. We also want to extend and encourage students to enter and compete on their entering the profession of digital media.

If an entrant is recognized as a winner, there is no additional fee. Many other awards programs charge an acceptance fee. With the NY Product Design Awards, once you've won, you've won; there is no additional fee.

If a winner decides they want a statuette, the NY Product Design Awards makes them available for purchase and orders them from its manufacturer. This offering process allows the Awards to maintain low entry fees and prevent non-winners’ subsidy of winners.

Some winners choose not to receive a physical memento. Their priority is the prestige of recognition and the exposure the comes with it. Others choose to order statuettes for individuals on their creative team as well as their client. It's all up to the winners!

Merchandise shipping time

Winner awards are custom-made. Statuettes will ship directly from the manufacturer. Please allow 6 - 12 weeks for the statuettes. Shipping time may be longer during off-season. Statuettes may be ordered from our online store once winners are announced.

Note: Certain countries might impose customs / duty fees and taxes for imported items. We have no control over these charges, and we suggest to consult your country's customs department.

We strongly recommend recording a video while opening your package, including any enclosed certificate documents. This video is essential in the event you need to file a replacement or damage claim. Without this, we may not be able to proceed with your request.

We’d much rather keep the cost of our submissions and products low, so our winners can choose to spend money on marketing that generates real-world value.

NY PRODUCT DESIGN AWARDS
Honoring Talented Product Designers, Design Teams & Manufacturers Worldwide.

The NY Product Design Awards was launched to honor the efforts of talented product designers, design teams, and manufacturers from all over the world, whose designs have made daily living that much better with their practical and ingenious creations.

Recognizing that excellence exists in various forms in the design industry, the NY Product Design Awards intends to celebrate excellence in all its facets that are found in design work and its designers.

A well-thought-out design is both functional and aesthetically pleasing, and all-in-all improves one’s daily life. Said designs can stem from different people’s personal projects, commercial engagements or researches. To the NY Product Design Awards, these are the individuals we wish to honor.

FEATURED JURY MEMBERS

Meet the world-class creatives, designers, and experts who formed the NY Product Design Awards jury panel.

The Awards is judged by members of the IAA Judging Council, a group of leading industry professionals. Showcase your brilliant projects and capture the attention of our esteemed panel of judges today!

United States

Yafei Zhang

Associate Principal / BIM Lead / Adjunct Professor, Kohn Pedersen Fox; Kean University

Yafei Zhang, AIA, LEED AP, is a licensed New Jersey architect and Associate Principal at Kohn Pedersen Fox (KPF) with over a decade of experience leading corporate interiors and mixed-use projects from concept through construction.

Yafei’s expertise spans the full project lifecycle, from feasibility and design development to construction and post-occupancy evaluation. She leads multidisciplinary consultant teams to deliver high-performance, sustainable interiors for leading corporate clients, including Meta, ensuring projects meet design, budget, and schedule goals.

Her notable projects include Meta 50 Hudson Yards, the Bund Fosun Center, and the University of Michigan Center for Innovation, reflecting her ability to turn complex requirements into cohesive, high-performing spaces. With strong expertise in Revit and BIM-driven workflows, Yafei drives project efficiency through data-informed coordination and delivery. She also serves as National Education Lead (North America) and Regional Lead (New York) for Women in BIM, advocating for leadership diversity and innovation across the AEC industry.

Bulgaria

Vasil Velchev

Media & Product Life Cycle Manager, Europe, Ideal Standard International NV

Vasil Velchev has amassed over a decade of experience as a graphic designer within global organizations. Initially, his foray into product design was more of a hobby, but as his creations gained international recognition and garnered awards, his confidence in his ideas grew.

Several years ago, He initiated the MAGMA project, a platform aimed at consolidating his diverse creations under one umbrella. The project's name is symbolic, representing the designer's belief that every original idea should remain in a constant "liquid" state, continuously evolving to explore its full potential and staying vibrant until it transforms into a tangible product.

Velchev also serves as a valued member of the Grand Jury for prestigious awards. His remarkable talent has earned him approximately 25 international awards across various categories, with a primary focus on furniture design, recognized by design awards worldwide.


Australia

Alexandre Collares

Creative Art Director, Ogilvy Australia

Alexandre de Mello Collares is a prominent creative art director of Ogilvy Australia.

Collares possesses more than a decade of experience in the advertising industry, having worked in both large and mid-sized agencies in the Australian and Brazilian markets, including DDB, McCann, Geometry Global, and Fox Sports, among others. Throughout his career, he has had the opportunity to collaborate with esteemed clients such as KFC, Pizza Hut, Coca-Cola, McDonald's, Fox Sports, SC Johnson, Westpac, and numerous others, allowing him to accumulate knowledge and exchange insights with a diverse and talented group of professionals.

He remains consistently focused and self-motivated, excelling both individually and in teamwork. With a profound passion for creative ideas and meticulous execution, he anticipates that his finest project is yet to come.

2025
Virtual Recognition + Winner Highlights
NY AWARDS STATUETTE

Ava: A Symbol of Design Excellence & Creation

Inspired by Galatea, a statue carved of ivory coming to life, the statuette is shaped in the form of a female humanoid, with elements that strongly affirms its non-biological origins.

The statuette is titled “Ava”, a variation of Eve, the first woman found in Abrahamic religions. Eve came to be as soon as life was breathed into her. In that regard, we recognize that designers are able to breathe life into their works, and give way to something truly great.

The design of the statuette is aimed to invoke a sense of pristine clarity with its frame clad in ivory white, while its golden embellishments effectively communicate a sense of elegance and divinity.

All of these elements culminate in symbols of excellence, creation, and the unbounded future that is in store. Much like Pygmalion – the sculptor of Galatea, whose excellence shaped his future, we too hope that this statuette symbolizes designers whose works shape the path they walk in their own futures.
